title.en: Where is my package? (Inquiring about deliveries)
title.ko: 언제쯤 도착하는지 알 수 있을까요? (배달/택배 문의하기)

description.en: Is your delivery late? Learn how to check the status politely and effectively using phrases like "언제쯤 도착하는지 알 수 있을까요?"
description.ko: 택배나 음식 배달이 늦어질 때! 기분 나쁘지 않게 진행 상황을 묻는 정중한 리액션을 배워봅니다.

tags: Situation. Life. Delivery. Inquiry. Polite. Request. Season 2

content.speaker: John
content.emotion: 배가 고픈 듯 힘없는 목소리로 바로 시작
content.text: Mina, I ordered fried chicken an hour ago, but it’s still not here.

content.speaker: John
content.text: I want to call the restaurant and ask "Where is my food?" but I don't want to sound like a "Karen."

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 배고플 때 기다리는 건 정말 힘들죠! 이해해요.
content.translation: Haha, waiting when you're hungry is really hard! I understand.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 땐..., "왜 안 와요?" 라고 묻기보다 조금 더 부드럽게 확인을 요청하는 게 좋아요.
content.translation: In that case, it's better to request a check softly rather than asking "Why isn't it coming?"

content.speaker: John
content.text: Is there a phrase that sounds like "Could you let me know when it might arrive?"

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네! 가장 세련된 표현은..., "언제쯤 도착하는지 알 수 있을까요?" 라고 말하는거예요.
content.translation: Yes! The most sophisticated expression is "Could I know around when it will arrive?"

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 오늘은 택배나 배달 상황에서 내 물건을 똑똑하게 챙기는 리액션들을 배워볼게요!
content.translation: Today, we'll learn reactions to smartly check on your items in delivery situations!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 언제쯤 도착하는지 알 수 있을까요?

content.speaker: John
content.text: This sounds very indirect and polite.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요! "언제 와요?" 보다 훨씬 정중하고 조심스럽게 들리죠.
content.translation: Right! It sounds much more polite and careful than "When is it coming?"

insight.title: 언제쯤 도착하는지 알 수 있을까요?
insight.description: 'Could I know around when it will arrive?'
insight.usage_tip: 언제(When) + 쯤(Around) + 도착하는지(Whether it arrives) + 알 수 있을까요?(Could I know?). Using 'ㄹ 수 있을까요' makes the question very soft and non-confrontational.

content.speaker: John
content.text: What if I just want them to check the status first?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 땐..., "확인 좀 부탁드릴게요" 라고 말해보세요.
content.translation: In that case, try saying "Please check it for me."

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "빨리 해달라" 는 말을 아주 예의 바르게 하는 방법이에요.
content.translation: It's a very polite way of saying "Please do it quickly."

insight.title: 확인 좀 부탁드릴게요
insight.usage_tip: 확인(Check) + 좀(Please/A bit) + 부탁드릴게요(I request/ask). This is the most standard phrase for asking for any kind of service check in Korea.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay, let's jump in. My chicken is 20 minutes overdue. I'm calling the restaurant.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네. 행복치킨입니다! 무엇을 도와드릴까요?
content.translation: Yes, this is Happy Chicken! How can I help you?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 안녕하세요. 1시간 전에 주문했는데요. 음식이 아직 안 와서 전화드렸어요.
content.translation: Hello. I ordered an hour ago. I'm calling because the food hasn't arrived yet.

insight.title: 전화드렸어요
insight.usage_tip: Using the honorific '드렸어요' makes the reason for your call sound very polite.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 아! 정말 죄송합니다! 지금 비가 와서 배달이 조금 지연되고 있네요.
content.translation: Ah, I'm truly sorry! The delivery is being delayed a bit because it's raining now.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그렇군요. 언제쯤 도착하는지 알 수 있을까요?
content.translation: Ah, I see. Could I know around when it will arrive?

insight.title: 언제쯤
insight.usage_tip: Adding '쯤' (around) shows you are flexible and not demanding an exact second.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 기사님이 5분 전에 출발하셨어요! 10분 안에는 도착할 거예요.
content.translation: The driver left 5 minutes ago! It will arrive within 10 minutes.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 네 알겠습니다. 확인 좀 부탁드릴게요. 감사합니다!
content.translation: Yes, I understand. Please check on it for me. Thank you!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Phew! It was much better to ask politely. I think the staff was more willing to help me out.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 한국에서는 독촉할 때도 예의를 지키면 더 좋은 서비스를 받을 수 있답니다.
content.translation: Right. In Korea, even when you're rushing someone, you can get better service if you stay polite.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "delivery" reactions one last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 언제쯤 도착하는지 알 수 있을까요?
content.translation: Could I know around when it will arrive?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 확인 좀 부탁드릴게요!
content.translation: Please check it for me!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 배송 상태 좀 알 수 있을까요?
content.translation: Could I know the shipping status?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 아직 못 받았는데 확인해 주시겠어요?
content.translation: I haven't received it yet, could you check it?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 문 앞에 두고 문자 주세요!
content.translation: Please leave it at the door and text me!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 오늘 택배나 배달을 기다리고 있다면, 당황하지 말고 정중하게 물어보세요.
content.translation: Everyone, if you're waiting for a package or delivery today, don't panic and ask politely.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "언제쯤 도착하는지 알 수 있을까요?"
content.translation: Around when will it arrive?

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 기차가 정시에 역에 도착했어요. (The train arrived at the station on time.)
사고 때문에 지하철 운행이 지연되고 있어요. (Subway operations are being delayed due to an accident.)
요즘은 앱으로 배달을 시키는 게 정말 편해요. (These days, ordering delivery via an app is really convenient.)
어제 주문한 택배가 오늘 벌써 왔네요! (The courier I ordered yesterday came already today!)
운송장 번호로 택배 위치를 조회할 수 있어요. (You can track the location of your package with the tracking number.)
일이 늦어져서 상사가 빨리 하라고 독촉했어요. (The work was delayed, so the boss urged me to do it quickly.)
